name: Moranbah Solar Farm
notable: CIS T7 award May 2026 (171 MW solar + 100 MW / 4hr BESS). Zero-E Australia is subsidiary of Spanish Grupo Cobra (ACS Group). Near Coppabella in Bowen Basin. AEMO 5.3.4A connection approval secured pre-award (145 MWac inverter rating per pv-tech; DCCEEW cites 171 MW; likely DC vs AC distinction). AC-coupled hybrid with grid-forming inverters. Grupo Cobras first Australian clean-energy asset. Cultural Heritage + Shared Benefits agreements with Barada Barna Traditional Owners. Created 2026-07-18 to close v3.22.2 data gap.

scheme_contracts.execution_notes: AEMO 5.3.4A connection approval secured pre-award. AC-coupled hybrid with grid-forming inverters. Cultural Heritage + Shared Benefits agreements with Barada Barna Traditional Owners. Pre-FID at ~2 months post-award.

operational_notes: CIS T7 award May 2026 (171 MW solar + 100 MW / 4hr BESS integrated hybrid). Zero-E Australia is subsidiary of Spanish Grupo Cobra (ACS Group — same ultimate parent as Pacific Partnerships / CIMIC / HOCHTIEF which won Hopeland Solar T1). Near Coppabella in Queensland's Bowen Basin. AEMO 5.3.4A connection approval secured pre-award (145 MWac inverter rating per pv-tech article; DCCEEW T7 media release cites 171 MW — likely DC vs AC rating distinction). AC-coupled hybrid with grid-forming inverters. Grupo Cobra's first Australian clean-energy asset. Cultural Heritage + Shared Benefits agreements signed with Barada Barna Traditional Owners. Up to 250 construction jobs. Grupo Cobra's second T7 win alongside Gunning Solar Hybrid (NSW). Created 2026-07-18 to close v3.22.2 data gap.

timeline_events.title: CIS Tender 7 award (171 MW + 100 MWh)
timeline_events.detail: Awarded CIS Tender 7 — NEM Generation. Grupo Cobra's second T7 win.
